title: Quiz: Modern Portfolio Theory
description: Test your understanding of the Markowitz framework and mean-variance optimization

questions.question: What is the key insight of Modern Portfolio Theory?

options.text: Higher returns always require higher risk
Portfolio risk depends on correlations between assets, not just individual risks
All investors should hold the same portfolio

questions.explanation: MPT's key insight is that diversification reduces risk because portfolio risk depends on how assets move together (correlations), not just individual asset risks.

questions.question: What is the efficient frontier?

options.text: The set of all possible portfolios
Portfolios with maximum return for each risk level

questions.explanation: The efficient frontier represents optimal portfolios that offer the highest expected return for each level of risk (or lowest risk for each return level).

questions.question: How is portfolio variance calculated?

options.text: w'Σw where Σ is the covariance matrix
Square root of the sum of squared weights

questions.explanation: Portfolio variance is σ²_p = w'Σw, a quadratic form that accounts for both variances and covariances between assets.

questions.question: What special property does the minimum variance portfolio have?

options.text: Lowest possible volatility among all portfolios

questions.explanation: The minimum variance portfolio (MVP) has the lowest possible volatility among all possible portfolios, sitting at the leftmost point of the efficient frontier.

questions.question: In MPT, two assets with perfect positive correlation (ρ=1) can be combined to reduce risk.
questions.explanation: False. When correlation is 1, there is no diversification benefit. Risk reduction through diversification only works when assets have imperfect correlation (ρ < 1).

questions.question: What is the tangent portfolio?

options.text: The portfolio with highest Sharpe ratio

questions.explanation: The tangent portfolio is the point on the efficient frontier with the highest Sharpe ratio - it lies where the capital allocation line from the risk-free rate is tangent to the efficient frontier.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT an assumption of Modern Portfolio Theory?
questions.explanation: MPT assumes markets are efficient (not predictable). It assumes normal returns, risk-averse investors, and frictionless markets with no transaction costs.

questions.question: Which constraints can make portfolio optimization NP-hard and potentially benefit from quantum computing? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Maximum of k assets allowed (cardinality constraint)

questions.explanation: Cardinality constraints (select k from n), integer constraints, and transaction costs make the problem non-convex and potentially NP-hard. The simple sum-to-one constraint is linear and easy to handle.
